author: Quoia75
msg:
I'm not understanding the "hard work" stance here or the comparison that keeps being made between the input of a UNO or a ULO. Both are vital to the network. Both play a specific role. And lets be honest, installing an app, maintaining good up time and completing a few manual tasks is not like toiling in a mine. 

I think this POV is coming again from mistakingly believing that UNOs are passive investors. 

This POV also diminishes the outlay of work the UNO had to produce to acquire the initial investment needed to purchase the nodes to begin with. Just based on a few stories I've read within this community, I'm assuming most here are not wealthy and had to make some sort of sacrifice to purchase a node or nodes. That's time & money invested.

Depending on the quality of the customer care you intend to provide to your ULOs or the effort you will put forth in marketing, the UNO role will involve some actual work and expenditure. It's clear the quality of the UX will vary from UNO to UNO.

author: Depins
msg:
Pros and cons. Everyone can't be happy. 

Fact is, people that are marketing currently are somewhat disadvantaged by having a marketplace undercutting them. 

By capping the marketplace those marketers are encouraged and incentivised to continue marketing. 

It also encourages others to market as well. 

All of these marketing efforts will have overflow. The overflow benefits Everyone including those not marketing. 

Suppose you wanted to use the market and do 10/90 to undercut everyone and "compete"
Now if your licenses are capped at 50/50, just 1 license leased is worth what 5  would be worth if leased at 10/90. 
So you retain more of your license supply. Bot bad considering you're not marketing yourself 

And you still can actively market as well. 

So the free market purest that doesnt want to do marketing is only disadvantaged in that they can't get all their licenses leased before everyone else by using aggressive pricing strategies 

But technically they still can they just have to be a little clever about it and put in some work. 

It's not possible to make everyone happy. Not having a cap now would be disadvantaged to those currently making an effort to bring in more ULO'S. 

I think this structure is worth giving it a chance.

author: Gwims
msg: You know I don’t believe these low uno leases would remain if a license suddenly starts making 1k a month.  My initial baseline is 70/30 privately. Information is key I think I even tell them about the market place, the splits that are available there and give that $1000 scenario. I explain that more licences will mean more money over time also each additional license I lower my share by 5%  and I cap it at 55% to uno. Even with all that my baseline on market place is 70/30 and my licences are being taken even with those 10 90 splits around. The problem with those splits is I know UlOs will get rugged people that have done it have said as much in previous threads I’ve read. As Calus said there are different users of the licenses some are content to sit back and get the passive, they won’t be that bothered with opt  in tasks. Others they will be on it, those guys I normally say to them start with 5 licences I paint the picture of a potential once everything is established. I also set people’s expectations from the beginning giving a longer time frame and what current rewards we are getting.  It’s a business we are running ourselves at the end of the day some will depend on market place some most of my licences are strangers I’ve just spoken to around me. Lol from my experience close friends and family ask you a million questions and still don’t download the app. I think all this will change when rewards are more significant, the first part the building is always harder
